summary: The IDF demolished a large Hezbollah underground tunnel facility exceeding 200 meters in length and 25 meters in depth in the southern Lebanon village of Majdal Zoun, which was used to assemble, store, and launch Iranian-made UAVs. An IDF soldier, Sgt. David Hazut, was killed during a Hezbollah encounter in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, and the Hezbollah operative involved was subsequently located and eliminated. Iran and the US have agreed to halt attacks on each other and plan technical meetings in Doha to resolve the Strait of Hormuz dispute, while the IRGC has attacked at least two vessels in the Gulf.
key_points: IDF demolished a Hezbollah underground tunnel in Majdal Zoun, southern Lebanon, over 200 meters long and deeper than 25 meters, used for Iranian-made UAV assembly, storage, and launch. (Source: @manniefabian/58700)

IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Jihad Ghannam terrorist Zarah Barhoum Abu Salem in northern Gaza, who infiltrated Israel on October 7 and participated in abducting Israeli civilians. (Source: @New_security8200/202147)
IDF soldier Sgt. David Hazut was killed and another lightly wounded in a Hezbollah encounter in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon; the operative was later located and eliminated. (Source: @pkpoi/143020)
Iran and US agreed to halt attacks on each other and plan technical meetings in Doha in the coming days to resolve the Strait of Hormuz dispute. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74500)
IRGC attacked at least two vessels in the Persian Gulf in the past two days, with US military responding on Trump's orders. (Source: @VahidOnline/76727)
IDF struck three Hezbollah command centers in Nabatieh and Mayfadoun areas in southern Lebanon and destroyed a Hezbollah rocket launcher. (Source: @manniefabian/58704)
IDF conducted artillery and helicopter gunfire on the village of Abdin and its surroundings in southern Syria; Syria condemned the attacks at the UN. (Source: @yediotnews25/132038)
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi met Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zayadi in Baghdad and warned the US against creating parallel arrangements for the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @asafroz/50824)

sections.executive_summary: The IDF demolished a large Hezbollah underground tunnel facility exceeding 200 meters in length and 25 meters in depth in the southern Lebanon village of Majdal Zoun, which was used to assemble, store, and launch Iranian-made UAVs. An IDF soldier was killed during a Hezbollah encounter in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, and the operative involved was subsequently located and eliminated. Iran and the US have agreed to halt attacks on each other and plan technical meetings in Doha to resolve the Strait of Hormuz dispute.

points: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi stated the Strait of Hormuz will reopen within 30 days under Iran's exclusive management per the memorandum of understanding with the US. (Source: @VahidOnline/76727)
IRGC attacked at least two vessels in the Persian Gulf in the past two days, drawing US military responses on Trump's orders. (Source: @VahidOnline/76727)
Iran and US agreed to halt attacks on each other and plan technical meetings in Doha in the coming days to resolve the Hormuz dispute. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74500)
Iranian President Pezeshkian announced $6 billion of Iran's frozen funds in Qatar are expected to be released under the agreement with the US. (Source: @N12chat/215132)

Araghchi met Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zayadi in Baghdad and warned the US that creating parallel arrangements for the Strait of Hormuz would only complicate matters and delay reopening. (Source: @asafroz/50824)
Araghchi received a hero's welcome at the Imam Hussein shrine in Karbala, Iraq. (Source: @presstv/196074)

IDF demolished a Hezbollah underground tunnel in Majdal Zoun, southern Lebanon, over 200 meters long and deeper than 25 meters, used for Iranian-made UAV assembly, storage, and launch; Israel updated the US beforehand. (Source: @manniefabian/58700)

IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Jihad Ghannam terrorist Zarah Barhoum Abu Salem in northern Gaza, who infiltrated Israel on October 7 and participated in abducting Israeli civilians. (Source: @New_security8200/202147)

IDF soldier Sgt. David Hazut was killed and another lightly wounded in a Hezbollah encounter in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon; the operative was later located and eliminated in a nearby building. (Source: @pkpoi/143020)
IDF struck three Hezbollah command centers in Nabatieh and Mayfadoun areas in southern Lebanon and destroyed a Hezbollah rocket launcher. (Source: @manniefabian/58704)
IDF conducted artillery and helicopter gunfire on the village of Abdin and its surroundings in southern Syria. (Source: @yediotnews25/132038)

IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Jihad Ghannam terrorist Zarah Barhoum Abu Salem in northern Gaza, who infiltrated Israel on October 7 and participated in abducting Israeli civilians from their homes. (Source: @New_security8200/202147)

sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo remains elevated across multiple theaters. In southern Lebanon, Hezbollah continues to engage IDF forces directly, as evidenced by the fatal encounter in Dir Siran, while the IDF is systematically dismantling Hezbollah underground infrastructure such as the Majdal Zoun tunnel. The IRGC's attacks on at least two vessels in the Persian Gulf represent a direct threat to maritime security, though the US-Iran agreement to halt attacks and hold Doha talks suggests a potential de-escalation pathway. In southern Syria, Israeli strikes on Abdin and the Syrian government's formal UN complaint indicate continued cross-border friction.
sections.regional_response: The Israeli-Lebanese framework agreement is being tested on the ground, with the IDF reserving the right to enter and verify Lebanese army dismantlement of Hezbollah infrastructure after previous instances of false reporting. Syria has escalated diplomatically by formally appealing to the UN to end Israeli violations and uphold the Disengagement of Forces Agreement. Iran's diplomatic engagement with Iraq, including Araghchi's meeting with Prime Minister al-Zayadi and his Karbala visit, signals an effort to maintain regional alliances while negotiating with the US over the Strait of Hormuz.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Majdal Zoun tunnel demolition reveals Hezbollah's continued reliance on deep underground infrastructure for Iranian-made UAV operations, suggesting a broader network may exist. The Dir Siran encounter demonstrates Hezbollah's maintained capability to inflict casualties on IDF forces during routine operations. The IRGC's vessel attacks in the Gulf, followed by the Doha talks agreement, indicate Tehran is using calibrated military pressure as leverage in negotiations. The discrepancy between Lebanese army reporting and actual Hezbollah infrastructure removal, as noted by i24, remains a verification concern for the ceasefire framework.
